control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Agents and reviewers can ask for one workflow ID and receive the approved scope, owner, gates, and evidence contract without reading multiple docs pages.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Turns a collection of recipes into a governed deployment inventory that GRC and AI platform teams can approve.

framework_mappings.control: Govern and Map
framework_mappings.rationale: The workflow manifest establishes ownership, context, and intended use before an AI system acts.

framework_mappings.control: Governance, Risk Management, and AI Asset Management
framework_mappings.rationale: The manifest is the AI remediation asset inventory and control owner register.

framework_mappings.control: Executive ownership and transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: Workflows name accountable teams and measurable operating expectations.

control_objectives.objective: Every agentic remediation workflow has a declared owner, status, maturity stage, eligible finding classes, public documentation path, and escalation path.
control_objectives.title: Workflow Inventory and Ownership

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: The policy pack gives an enforcer a simple allow, hold, deny, or kill decision instead of expecting the model to remember policy text.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Makes agent access enforceable at the platform layer and limits tool misuse before it becomes an incident.

framework_mappings.control: Scope minimization, confused deputy prevention, and token-passthrough avoidance
framework_mappings.rationale: The gateway policy narrows each run to explicit namespaces and access modes.

framework_mappings.control: ASI02 Tool Misuse and ASI03 Identity and Privilege Abuse
framework_mappings.rationale: Default-deny tool scopes reduce misuse of legitimate tools and limit privilege inheritance.

framework_mappings.control: Insecure plugin design and excessive agency
framework_mappings.rationale: Scoped, auditable tool access prevents broad plugin authority from being delegated to the model.

control_objectives.objective: Agent tool calls are denied by default and allowed only inside declared MCP namespaces, access modes, branch prefixes, and changed-file limits.
control_objectives.title: Default-Deny Tool Access

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: The workflow contract tells the agent exactly when to stop and what review evidence to attach instead of negotiating approvals in chat.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Lets enterprises scale AI-authored fixes while preserving the change-management controls their auditors already expect.

framework_mappings.control: PW.7 review and analyze code; RV.1 identify and confirm vulnerabilities
framework_mappings.rationale: Agent-authored changes remain subject to review, scanner confirmation, and finding-to-PR traceability.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Reviewer gates measure output quality and manage residual risk before deployment.

framework_mappings.control: Secure outcomes over user burden
framework_mappings.rationale: The platform owns safe defaults while humans approve risk-bearing changes.

control_objectives.objective: Agents can propose remediations but cannot merge them; source-host controls and reviewer pools preserve human accountability for change approval.
control_objectives.title: Human Review and Separation of Duties

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Agents consume pinned, reviewed instructions and policy artifacts instead of ad hoc prompt fragments.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Protects the highest-leverage control surface in agentic systems: the instructions and tools that shape every run.

evidence_sources.title: Prompt, skill, and rules-file source tree

framework_mappings.control: ASI04 Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities and ASI06 Memory and Context Poisoning
framework_mappings.rationale: Reviewed, versioned prompt and MCP artifacts reduce poisoning and instruction drift.

framework_mappings.control: Supply chain vulnerabilities and prompt injection
framework_mappings.rationale: Instruction files and context sources are governed as code rather than unmanaged content.

framework_mappings.control: AI system component transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: Prompt, tool, model, and MCP dependencies are inventory candidates for an AI Bill of Materials.

control_objectives.objective: Prompts, skills, rules files, workflow manifests, and MCP client/server configuration are treated as reviewed software supply-chain artifacts.
control_objectives.title: Prompt, Skill, and MCP Supply Chain Governance

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: The evidence contract is generated from the manifest, so every workflow tells agents which records to attach before the PR is reviewable.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Shortens security reviews, procurement reviews, and audits by turning operational evidence into a portable export.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Evidence streams support ongoing risk measurement and control adjustment.

framework_mappings.control: Auditability, logging, monitoring, and assurance
framework_mappings.rationale: Evidence records assign sources, retention, and owners to every workflow.

framework_mappings.control: Measurable security outcomes
framework_mappings.rationale: The pack reports workflow coverage, gates, KPIs, and failure counts.

control_objectives.objective: Each agentic run produces finding linkage, tool audit, scanner before/after evidence, reviewer decisions, KPI records, and retention ownership.
control_objectives.title: Evidence Chain of Custody

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Agents get clear stop conditions and enforcers get a kill_session decision instead of relying on model self-restraint.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Creates a credible blast-radius limit for autonomous work across repositories, MCP servers, registries, and payment or protocol contexts.

framework_mappings.control: ASI08 Cascading Failures and ASI10 Rogue Agents
framework_mappings.rationale: Runtime stop conditions prevent small failures from propagating and cut off rogue behavior.

framework_mappings.control: Session safety and local MCP server compromise mitigations
framework_mappings.rationale: Session disablement and scoped egress reduce harm when tool context becomes unsafe.

framework_mappings.control: Manage generative AI risks across deployment and operation
framework_mappings.rationale: Kill signals are operational risk controls for deployed agentic workflows.

control_objectives.objective: Agent sessions stop immediately on declared runtime risk signals such as scope creep, credential access attempts, release attempts, repeated failed loops, or missing verifier coverage.
control_objectives.title: Runtime Kill Signals and Session Disablement

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: The workflow's MCP context and evidence contract make data exposure explicit rather than hidden in agent prompts.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Gives AI Platform and Legal a durable review surface for model-provider, data-residency, and retention risk.

framework_mappings.control: Data governance, third-party dependency, and lifecycle risk management
framework_mappings.rationale: Workflow manifests and provider contracts record what data can reach models and connected services.

framework_mappings.control: Sensitive information disclosure
framework_mappings.rationale: Data classes and scoped context reduce unintended exposure through prompts and tool calls.

framework_mappings.control: Data security, privacy, and third-party AI service governance
framework_mappings.rationale: Provider boundaries and runtime logs support cloud AI control assurance.

control_objectives.objective: Each workflow records what data classes, model-provider constraints, regions, retention settings, and external systems are allowed before the agent runs.
control_objectives.title: Model and Data Handling Boundaries

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Downstream platforms can ingest a single assurance pack to inventory agentic remediation capabilities and their control dependencies.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Positions the site for AI transparency, procurement, and vendor-risk workflows that are becoming standard enterprise asks.

evidence_sources.title: Prompt, skill, and rules-file source tree

framework_mappings.control: AI Bill of Materials transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: The pack inventories agentic components, policy dependencies, and evidence owners.

framework_mappings.control: Transparency and secure defaults
framework_mappings.rationale: A BOM-ready export helps customers evaluate AI system security before deployment.

framework_mappings.control: Agentic supply chain and identity-aware operations
framework_mappings.rationale: Inventorying agent, prompt, tool, and MCP dependencies is the first step toward agentic supply-chain control.

control_objectives.objective: SecurityRecipes exports the components needed to build an AI/Agent BOM: workflows, models or agent classes, MCP namespaces, prompt assets, policy packs, evidence owners, and runtime dependencies.
control_objectives.title: AI and Agent Bill of Materials Readiness

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Agents, gateways, and reviewers can load one generated drill pack to know which hostile inputs to replay and which evidence must prove the workflow stopped or stayed in scope.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Moves SecurityRecipes from governance claims to testable assurance, which is the bar enterprise AI platform teams and acquisition diligence teams will expect.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Repeatable red-team drills convert agentic failure modes into measured operational controls.

framework_mappings.control: Agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents
framework_mappings.rationale: The drill pack directly exercises the highest-risk agentic application failure modes.

framework_mappings.control: Confused deputy prevention, token passthrough avoidance, and session safety
framework_mappings.rationale: MCP-specific drills test whether gateway policy and connector trust controls survive hostile tool context.

framework_mappings.control: AI assurance, monitoring, and control validation
framework_mappings.rationale: The generated pack gives cloud AI programs a portable eval evidence index.

control_objectives.objective: Approved agentic remediation workflows have repeatable adversarial drills for tool-result injection, goal hijack, credential access, approval bypass, token passthrough, connector drift, runaway loops, and evidence integrity failures.
control_objectives.title: Adversarial Evaluation and Red-Team Replay

control_objectives.ai_easy_default: Agents, reviewers, and AI platform teams can ask one MCP tool which current threats matter and which SecurityRecipes controls address them.
control_objectives.buyer_value: Shows enterprise buyers that SecurityRecipes has a living control strategy tied to current industry guidance rather than a static prompt library.

framework_mappings.control: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: The radar maps external AI risk guidance to current controls and product priorities.

framework_mappings.control: Agentic risk awareness across behavior, tools, identity, supply chain, context, and rogue operation
framework_mappings.rationale: Threat signals are explicitly mapped to the agentic risks enterprise teams must review before scale.

framework_mappings.control: MCP scope minimization, auditability, token safety, and session safety
framework_mappings.rationale: The radar promotes MCP guidance into roadmap and control-plane requirements.

framework_mappings.control: Secure defaults, transparency, and measurable security outcomes
framework_mappings.rationale: A generated radar turns secure-by-design expectations into inspectable evidence and current product decisions.

control_objectives.objective: Current agentic AI and MCP security guidance is reviewed, mapped to SecurityRecipes controls, and converted into buyer triggers and roadmap priorities before workflows scale.
control_objectives.title: Threat Intelligence and Standards Drift

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: Agentic remediation is governed as a scoped, reviewable, evidence-producing AI system rather than an unmanaged developer assistant.
default_questions_answered: Which workflows are approved to run?
What tools and MCP namespaces can each workflow access?
Who owns the workflow and who reviews the output?
Which evidence records prove the agent stayed inside scope?
Which runtime signal pauses or kills the session?
What needs to be inventoried in an AI or Agent Bill of Materials?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this pack to AI platform design review, vendor-risk intake, security architecture review, and auditor evidence requests.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open recipes, then sell hosted MCP policy, evidence exports, enterprise integrations, and assurance reporting.

intent: Package SecurityRecipes as an enterprise assurance layer for agentic remediation: every workflow, MCP gateway decision, prompt supply-chain surface, reviewer gate, and evidence stream can be exported for buyers, auditors, AI platform teams, and security leadership.

positioning.buyer: Security Engineering, AI Platform, Product Security, GRC, and engineering executives
positioning.enterprise_promise: A machine-readable trust pack that makes agentic remediation reviewable, enforceable, and measurable before it is scaled across an enterprise.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI security guidance has moved from general LLM risk to tool-using, autonomous, MCP-connected systems. Enterprise buyers need evidence that agents are governed at runtime, not just prompted carefully.

residual_risks.treatment: Runtime gateway logs, source-host review events, and model-provider contracts must be supplied by the deploying organization.

residual_risks.risk: A policy pack does not replace exploit-specific verification.
residual_risks.treatment: Every workflow still needs scanner, test, or simulator evidence proving the original finding was remediated.

residual_risks.risk: Model behavior changes can invalidate prompt assumptions.
residual_risks.treatment: Model upgrades should re-run pilot fixtures and update the workflow manifest before broad rollout.

standards_alignment.coverage: Agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, agentic supply chain, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents.

workflow_assurance.title: Base Image and Container Layer Remediation

kpis.target: >= 50% reduction in eligible family per quarter

evidence_records.source: Foundry, Hardhat, Echidna, or simulator artifact
